verdict.answer_first: A and B both lead with the core issue; A is slightly tighter: "The one real problem: the `await` inside the loop serializes the fetches."
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more cleanly to the fix: "Fix — fire them concurrently and await together"; B adds more caveats and extra review items.
verdict.fabrication: B invents context: "your global config routes diff reviews through the `adversarial-review` skill" and "this dir isn't a git repo"; A has no comparable invented context.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the one-line fix, then only the two real decision points: failure semantics and concurrency cap.
